breakdown: Naming: Good descriptive test function names like test_runtime_bootstrap_invalidates_caches_and_uses_timeouts. Variables like commands, missing_modules are contextually clear. Some generic names (cmd, kwargs) but acceptable in test context. Error Handling: Tests properly verify error paths - subprocess.CalledProcessError handling, status_code 500 checks, error message assertions. Type Safety: Proper type hints throughout (list[list[str]], set[str], Path, tmp_path: Path). Mock objects created with type() are pragmatic for testing. Architecture: Clean separation - test files in tests/ directory, test_loc_script.py for script testing, runtime bootstrap tests for base module. Module boundaries respected. Test Coverage: This IS the test coverage - substantive test cases with real assertions, not stubs. Covers failure inference, bootstrap scenarios, fallback candidates, signature guard, cache invalidation. No empty test files. Documentation: Minimal - test function names are self-documenting but no docstrings or comments explaining WHY for complex test scenarios. No commented-out code.
key_changes: Added test_process_action_stream_infers_failure_without_explicit_success - tests error status inference from structured response with 500 status code
Added test_runtime_bootstrap_skips_numpy_downgrade_when_repair_not_needed - tests numpy version check logic
Added test_runtime_bootstrap_invalidates_caches_and_uses_timeouts - tests importlib.invalidate_caches() call and timeout kwargs
Added test_evidence_signature_guard_skips_external_server_runs - tests external server bypass logic
Added test_runtime_bootstrap_tries_fallback_package_candidates - tests fallback from onnxruntime-silicon to onnxruntime
Added tests/scripts/test_loc_script.py - new test file for loc.sh script with boundary-aware test detection
